sections.paragraph:
παραλλάσσω aor. impv. 2 sg. παράλλαξον and inf. παραλλάξαι LXX; pf. pass. ptc. παρηλλαγμένος (Trag., Hdt. et al.; PHib 27, 50 [III b.c.]; Sb 4947, 4; LXX) to change, with focus on being different, change παρηλλαγμένος strange, extraordinary, peculiar (Polyb. 2, 29, 1; 3, 55, 1; Diod S 14, 70, 4; 17, 90, 1; Plut., Thes. 16 [34, 3], Them. 124 [24, 3]; Lucian, Dial. Deor. 10, 2; TestSol 1:3 L; Philo, Poster. Cai. 9) διάλεκτος παρηλλαγμένη a peculiar language Dg 5:2.—M-M. s.v. παραλλαγή.

aor. aor. = aorist

impv. impv. = imperative

  1. sg. = singular

inf. inf. = infinitive

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PHib PHib = The Hibeh Papyri I–II—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 768
